description: The act of mindfully acknowledging a gift, an act of kindness, or a benefit received. It is seen as completing the circle of a gift, and participating in a cycle of generosity.
rationale: The word 'nalo' is sonically warm and open. The initial syllable 'na-' evokes a sense of genuine, present acknowledgement (Buddhist). The final syllable '-lo' represents the harmonious, circular flow of the gift being received (Solarpunk, Pre-industrial). The word frames gratitude as a joyful recognition of a completed cycle.
